canonical_name: Electrolux EWW 12480 W Washer-Dryer — User Manual (Phase 29 starter pack)/Troubleshooting/Drying does not complete/Service and policy
text: Service and policy
E10: Water supply fault
The appliance displays **E10** when it cannot fill water within the expected time. The flashing **Start/Pause** light also indicates the alarm.
Switch the appliance off and unplug it.
Check that the cold-water tap is fully open.
Inspect the inlet hose for kinks or pinching.
Clean the water-inlet filter at the tap connector if needed.
Restore power and restart the programme.
Warning: Do not attempt to disassemble the water valve.
E20: Water draining fault
The appliance displays **E20** when it cannot drain water within the expected time.
Switch the appliance off and unplug it.
Check that the drain hose is not kinked or pinched.
Clean the drain-pump filter (see "Cleaning the pump").
Empty the pump using the emergency-emptying procedure if the drum still contains water.
Restore power and restart the programme.

Warning: Use a shallow container to catch the drained water
when cleaning the pump filter.
E40: Door fault
The appliance displays **E40** when the door is not properly closed.
Open the door and check for laundry caught in the seal.
Close the door firmly until it latches.
Press **Start/Pause**.
If E40 persists, contact the Authorised Service Centre.

canonical_name: loading_laundry_and_dosing_detergent
text: Loading laundry and dosing detergent
Sort laundry by colour, fabric type, and wash symbol.
Check pockets for hard items and remove them.
Open the door and place laundry loosely in the drum.
Close the door until it latches.

Open the detergent drawer. Add main-wash detergent to the left compartment, pre-wash detergent (if used) to the middle compartment, and fabric softener to the right compartment up to the **MAX** mark.

canonical_name: selecting_a_programme
text: Selecting a programme
Turn the **programme selector** to the chosen programme.
Press the **Temperature** button to adjust if required.
Press the **Spin Reduction** button to adjust spin speed.
Press the **Automatic Dry** or **Drying Time** button if a dry phase is required.
Press **Start/Pause** to start.

canonical_name: time_manager
text: Time Manager
Choose a wash programme.
Press `+` or `–` to increase or decrease the wash time within the allowed range for the selected programme.
Press **Start/Pause**.

canonical_name: delay_start
text: Delay Start
Select a programme and any options.
Press the **Delay Start** button until the desired delay is shown.
Press **Start/Pause**. The display shows the time remaining until the cycle starts.

canonical_name: cancel_a_running_programme
text: Cancel a running programme
Hold the **Reset** position on the programme selector for several seconds, or press and hold **Start/Pause**.
The display indicates the programme has ended.
Open the door once the drum stops and the door indicator clears.

canonical_name: automatic_drying_after_washing
text: Automatic Drying after washing
Select a wash programme.
Press **Automatic Dry**.
Choose a drying degree (Extra Dry, Cupboard Dry, Iron Dry).
Press **Start/Pause**. The appliance washes, spins, and then dries automatically.

canonical_name: timed_drying_only
text: Timed Drying only
Turn the **programme selector** to the drying-only position.
Press **Drying Time** until the desired time is shown.
Press **Start/Pause**.

canonical_name: cleaning_the_dispenser_drawer
text: Cleaning the dispenser drawer
Pull the dispenser drawer out fully and lift it free.
Rinse the drawer under warm running water.
Use a small brush to clean the recess inside the appliance.
Refit the drawer.

canonical_name: cleaning_the_drain_pump
text: Cleaning the drain pump
Switch off and unplug the appliance.
Place a shallow tray under the pump access panel.
Open the panel; unscrew the pump filter slowly to drain.
Remove debris from the filter and impeller chamber.
Refit the filter, close the panel, and restore power.

Warning: The drained water can be hot. Wait until the
appliance has cooled.

canonical_name: emergency_emptying
text: Emergency emptying
Switch off the appliance.
Open the access panel at the bottom front.
Pull the emergency drain hose, place it over a low container, and remove the plug.
After all the water has drained, replace the plug, coil the hose, and close the panel.

canonical_name: cleaning_the_water_inlet_filter
text: Cleaning the water inlet filter
Close the water tap.
Unscrew the inlet hose from the tap.
Clean the small mesh filter inside the hose connector.
Reconnect the hose firmly and reopen the tap.
Specification | Value
Maximum wash load | 7 kg

Warning: Risk of electric shock. Unplug the appliance from
the mains before any cleaning or maintenance.

Hazard: Do not attempt self-repair of the washer-dryer. All
service, including pump, motor, control board, and heating
element work, must be carried out by an Authorised Service
Centre.

Warning: The door glass can be very hot during a wash or dry
cycle. Do not touch the door glass until it has cooled.

Hazard: Risk of fire — do not place items containing foam
rubber, latex, or rubberised backings in the dryer.

Warning: Do not dry items that have been treated with
volatile cleaning fluids, solvents, or petrol — risk of fire
and explosion.

Warning: Check pockets for gas lighters, coins, keys, and
other items before washing. Lighters in particular can cause
fire or appliance damage.

Warning: Risk of scalding from hot drained water — only use
the emergency-emptying procedure when the appliance has
cooled and the door is unlocked.

Warning: Do not overload the drum. Refer to the maximum-load
table for each programme.

Warning: Keep children and pets away from the appliance when
it operates. Never allow a child or pet to enter the drum.

Warning: Risk of water leakage — installation requires a
qualified plumber to connect the inlet and drain hoses.

Warning: Risk of electric shock — installation requires a
qualified electrician to connect the mains supply if a
hard-wired connection is used.

Warning: The drying cycle requires the cold-water tap to be
open. If the tap is closed the appliance halts and signals an
alarm.

Warning: Do not use a dosing ball in non-stop wash+dry
cycles. The dosing ball can be ejected into the drum during
drying and damage the appliance.

Warning: Do not dry delicate items, items containing rubber,
or items larger than the drum recommendation.

**Scope note.** This Markdown pack is a *Phase 29 scaffolded
starter* — it captures the highest-value safety, procedure,
control, error-code, spec, and troubleshooting content from the
real Electrolux washer-dryer manual (English content only;
Spanish duplicate ignored in this first pass) to demonstrate
that the `manual-graph-rag` architecture works on real PDF
text. Source PDF is local-only under
`data_local/real_manuals/raw/electrolux_washer_dryer/`.

Do not overload the drum. Refer to the maximum-load table for each programme. Overloading the drum reduces wash and spin performance, can damage the drum bearing, and increases vibration during spin. Do not exceed the maximum wash load or the maximum dry load for the selected programme.
The door glass can be very hot during a wash or dry cycle. Do not touch the door glass until it has cooled. The hot door glass risk is highest immediately after a hot wash or a drying cycle.
The drying cycle requires the cold-water tap to be open. If the tap is closed the appliance halts and signals an alarm.
Switch off and unplug the appliance before cleaning the dispenser drawer. Hot drained water can scald.
Switch off and unplug the appliance before cleaning the drain pump. The drained water can be hot. Place a shallow tray under the pump access panel before unscrewing the filter.
Close the water tap before unscrewing the inlet hose to clean the water inlet filter.
When the **Start/Pause** light flashes and `Err` is shown, the appliance has detected a fault that prevents safe operation.
Remedy: switch off, check the obvious causes (tap, hose, filter, door), and restart. If `Err` persists, contact the Authorised Service Centre.
The control panel contains:
**Programme selector** — turn to choose a wash or dry
programme.
**Temperature button** — sets wash temperature.
**Spin Reduction button** — reduces or disables spin.
**Automatic Dry button** — selects a drying degree.
**Drying Time button** — sets a timed dry.
**Delay Start button** — delays the programme start.
**Time Manager buttons (+/−)** — shorten or lengthen wash time.
**Start/Pause button** — starts or pauses the programme.
**Display** — shows status, faults, and time remaining.
**Extra Rinse light** — indicates an extra rinse is enabled.
Left compartment — **main wash detergent**.
Middle compartment — **pre-wash detergent** (if used).
Right compartment with cap — **fabric softener** up to the
MAX mark.
Possible causes: door not fully closed; Start/Pause not pressed; mains power off.
Remedy: close the door firmly, press Start/Pause, check the mains.
Water supply fault — see the E10 procedure above.
Water draining fault — see the E20 procedure above.
Door fault — see the E40 procedure above.
Possible causes: laundry unevenly distributed; transit bolts not removed at installation; appliance not levelled.
Remedy: pause the cycle, redistribute the laundry, check the levelling feet, and resume.
Possible cause: detergent overdosed.
Remedy: reduce detergent quantity; rinse the drum on an empty cycle.
Possible causes: cold-water tap closed; lint filter blocked; drying-degree set too low.
Remedy: open the tap, clean the lint trap (if accessible), and select a higher drying degree.
All electrical, motor, control-board, and heating-element
repairs must be carried out by an Authorised Service Centre.
The dispenser drawer, drain-pump filter, and water-inlet
filter are the only user-serviceable parts.
Do not attempt to bypass the door interlock under any
circumstances.
